key_positions: Aims to peak carbon dioxide emissions before 2030 and achieve carbon neutrality before 2060.
Emphasizes the principle of 'common but differentiated responsibilities' (CBDR) in global climate action.
Rapidly expanding domestic renewable energy capacity and green manufacturing capabilities.
red_lines: Will not accept absolute emission reduction targets mandated by developed nations.
Strongly opposes the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, viewing it as a protectionist trade barrier.
